avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Stability will remain good in many places. Hard and icy conditions will be prevalent. At first, localised deposits of windslab on some steep South aspects above 950m. Stronger winds and a change in direction may induce drifting later in the period and lead to new thin and weak windslab development on some steep North to North-East aspects above 950m.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: No precipitation is forecast. Remaining very cold. Light West-North-West winds freshening and becoming South- Westerly, then Southerly much later.
Observed Weather Influences: Some overnight snow showers then dry and cold with periods of bright sunshine. Light generally Northerly winds freshened by late morning.
ObservedSnowStability: Good stability, hard and icy in many locations. Limited drifting noted from late morning onwards. At the time of reporting localised pockets of soft new windslab were forming on a few steep Southerly aspects above 950m. Areas of shallow moderately bonded older windslab lie on some steep West to North-West facing aspects above the same altitude.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The old snowpack will be frozen, hard and stable, with pockets of shallow softer snow. Above 900 metres, areas of unstable windslab will be present on mainly South-West to North-West aspects. Accumulations will not be widespread but will remain poorly bonded and in places lie on a fragile thin crust. Later in the day some redistribution to more Northerly aspects will occur with new windslab forming. Cornices will be present on some high Westerly aspects. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Dry and cold through the period with the freezing level around 300 meters rising only slightly through the day. Overnight winds from West or South-Westerly directions increase fresh to strong from the South by the end of the day.
Observed Weather Influences: A bright and sunny day. Very cold with moderate North-Easterly winds. Minus 6 Celsius at around 1000 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing snowpack is frozen, hard and stable at all elevations with pockets of shallow softer snow. Areas of unstable windslab are present on South-West to North-West aspects mainly above 850 metres. These accumulations are generally shallow and not widespread but are poorly bonded and in places lye on a fragile crust. Small cornices are present on high Westerly aspects. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The older snowpack will remain firm and stable. Isolated and shallow areas of windslab will be present mainly on South-East through South to West aspects above 700 metres. There will be some redistribution of this snow onto North-West to North-East aspects in the afternoon. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: It will be cold and dry after some evening snow showers. Moderate North-West winds will back to the South by dawn and increase to fresh in the afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: It was a cold, dry, sunny morning with snow showers in the afternoon. Moderate winds were from the North.
ObservedSnowStability: In most places the snowpack is firm, frozen and stable. Isolated areas of shallow moderately bonded windslab are present mainly on South to West aspects above 700 metres. These areas are generally avoidable. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Little change is expected in the snowpack. Areas of moderately bonded windslab will remain. These areas not particularly extensive with South-West through West to North aspects above 900 metres being most affected. On other aspects, and below this level, the snowpack will generally be firm and icy. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Another cold but settled day is expected. The freezing level will be around 300 metres. Southerly winds will strengthen through the afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: It was a dry, sunny and cold day. After an overnight frost at all levels the freezing level rose to around 400 metres during the day. There was a fresh breeze from the North-East.
ObservedSnowStability: Much of the snowpack is firm and icy. However, some areas of windslab remain in sheltered locations above 1000 metres. Although these areas are not particularly extensive, some poorly bonded deposits remain.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Localised areas of moderately bonded windslab will be encountered in wind sheltered locations only, mainly South-East through South-West to North-West aspects above 800 metres. Gully tops and corrie rims most affected. Exposed locations will be wind scoured and icy.  The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Another bright and dry day is expected. Remaining cold initially with the freezing level moving up to 500 metres late afternoon. Fresh winds from the North-West easing late morning before backing to the South and strengthening.
Observed Weather Influences: A bright day with occasional cloud around the tops. Temperature was -6 degrees on the tops first thing. Strong winds from the North for most of the day.
ObservedSnowStability: New moderately bonded windslab is present, mainly on South-East through South-West to North-West aspects above 800 metres. Accumulations are limited in extent; poorest stability was found in sheltered locations around corrie rims, gully tops and steep convex features. Exposed terrain is icy. New cornices are fragile.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Above 950m widespread unstable windslab will be present on West to North aspects; this will be more localised on North-East and South to South-West aspects. Steep terrain such as coire headwalls, convexities and gullies will be most affected. Below 950m localised unstable windslab will remain on some steep banks and burn lines. Later in the day new windslab will develop above 900m on North-West to North-East aspects. New cornices will be fragile. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Dry conditions expected throughout the forecast period.  Fresh North-Westerly winds will back to become Southerly by the afternoon and increase to strong. The 300 metre freezing level will rise later to 450 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: Light snow showers on fresh Northerly winds.  The freezing level was around 400 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Localised unstable windslab has developed on Southerly aspects above 1000m. Widespread unstable windslab is present mainly above 950m on West to North aspects; more localised on North-East and South-West aspects. Steep terrain such as coire headwalls, convexities and gullies are most affected. Localised moderately bonded windslab is present on some steep slopes below 950m. Elsewhere above 1000m weaknesses persist beneath a widespread friable crust. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.
